statement.en: A symbolic grammar whose structural center is the empty symbol (śūnyatā) and whose self-application yields itself (śūnyatā(śūnyatā) = 0) is strictly more expressive than conventional symbolic grammars for modeling reflection, recursion, and foundational crisis. Conjectured: every self-referential paradox resolvable by ZCSG is resolvable in no weaker zero-positioned grammar.

reiTyping.rationale: ZCSG — Zero-Centric Symbolic Grammar (Paper 61, derived from Nagarjuna śūnyatā): formalizes śūnyatā(śūnyatā) = 0 and provides a self-referential symbolic grammar where the nil/empty element centrally organizes structure rather than being peripheral.

formalization.notes: Foundational framework; formal verification scope per-component
